title: Payment dispute reason catalogue
description: The 9 dispute reasons the module supports. Each documents the typical merchant defense strategy, provider-specific mapping (Stripe's dispute reason ↔ our enum ↔ Adyen's ↔ etc.), and whether evidence collection auto-includes specific fields.

dispute_reasons.description: The cardholder claims the transaction was unauthorized. Merchant defense: prove the customer authenticated (3DS2 result, IP + device fingerprint match to history, delivery/service confirmation).
The cardholder claims they were charged twice for the same purchase. Merchant defense: prove the charges are for distinct products/services or that a refund was already issued for the duplicate.
The cardholder claims they never received the product/service. Merchant defense: prove delivery (shipping tracking, service completion confirmation, session attendance record).
The cardholder claims the product/service was defective or not as described. Merchant defense: prove the product matched the description + no defect claims within the refund window.
The cardholder claims they canceled the subscription but was still charged. Merchant defense: prove the cancellation was received AFTER the charge date OR that no cancellation was received.
The cardholder claims they were promised a refund/credit that was never issued. Merchant defense: prove either that the credit WAS issued OR that no credit was owed.
Catch-all reason for disputes that don't fit a specific category. Merchant defense: provide all relevant evidence and describe the situation.

Customer contacted us directly + we escalated to dispute for internal tracking. Not a provider-initiated dispute. Reserved for the chargeback-listener integration when a Chargeback lands without our provider knowing first.
The cardholder doesn't recognize the merchant descriptor on their statement. Similar defense to fraudulent but usually resolves easily with a clarifying communication log + service documentation.

notes: The reason enum is the authoritative list. Adding a new reason = updating this catalogue + updating the PaymentDisputeReason enum + updating each provider's webhook handler to map the provider-native reason to our enum.
The provider_mapping documents the reverse-direction bridge — when a provider webhook fires with their native reason string, the WebhookHandler translates it to our enum.
auto_collected=false means CollectDisputeEvidenceJob skips auto-collection for this reason — ops manually assembles evidence.
The typical_defense_evidence list guides admin UI — the SDUI evidence submission form highlights which fields to focus on given the dispute reason.
